creator: raimeetravel
caption:
Was it worth flying 12hrs to ski in Japan vs. somewhere like Colorado in the states? It depends how you plan your trip BUT if you plan it like this, it definitely can be! 

#japan #skijapan #hakuba #niseko #skiseason

transcription.text: I heard it was cheaper to stay in Japan, so here's the full breakdown. First, my Lyft tickets in Japan cost me $150 for three days, full access to 10 different resorts. When comparing that cost to even some of the more affordable resorts in America, the cost difference is striking. Next, accommodation costs. I compared the same dates for Hakuba and a resort town in Colorado. For both destinations, I'm comparing the lowest prices that I found. So as you can see, there's also a huge difference here. Then for transportation, both destinations offer a free shuttle that'll take you around town and to the mountain. But you also have to consider the cost of getting from the main city to the resort town, so here are those costs. Then for gear rentals, I found that on average, it was about $100 cheaper to rent your gear in Japan versus similar resorts in Colorado. And I also wanted to mention food costs because in resort towns, it can be pricier, but I still found that generally the resort town in Japan was less expensive than the States. Finally, what about flight prices? I pulled some averages from some of the major US airports. My round trip business class flights only cost me about $200 though, because I used my points. Overall, when I added everything up, it seemed like it was about $1,000 cheaper to ski in Japan, even with flight costs. If you have any other questions about skiing in Japan, let me know.
